document_description: Academic paper reporting water quality measurements for river, drain, wastewater, or related aquatic samples.
extraction_scope: Extract the main quantitative water quality measurements from the paper's most relevant tables or figures and normalize them to one measurement per record using the fixed project-wide schema.
record_label: one measured water quality parameter value from a relevant source in the paper
records_description: Normalized water quality measurements extracted from papers with the fixed project-wide column set and one record per parameter value.
extraction_instructions: Prefer tables over running text whenever the same values appear in both places.
Extract one record per measured parameter value, not one wide row containing many pollutants.

Use the actual sampling location name, station name, drain name, city, stretch, or site description. Do not use legend-only IDs such as S1, P1, N1, or site_id unless the paper never provides a real name.
Split temporal information into Date, Month, Year, and Season when the paper provides those pieces. Leave unknown pieces null instead of combining everything into one field.
Use parameter for the pollutant or water quality indicator exactly as shown or in a clean standardized form close to the paper wording.
Preserve the reported cell content in actual_value when it is a single value, range, BDL/ND marker, or other reported value.
If the paper reports mean plus/minus SD, put the mean in mean and standard deviation in std_dev. Also preserve the original reported string in actual_value when useful.
Use source to record where the value came from, such as Table 2, Figure 3, Graph 1, Appendix Table A1, or Text.
Use notes for approximation, ambiguity, conversions, inferred units, merged cells, source quotes, or extraction caveats.
If a table contains several pollutants for the same location and date/season, create separate records for each pollutant.
preferred_table_signals: water quality or physicochemical parameter table
headers containing pollutant names, parameter names, indicators, or units
tables with locations, stations, sites, reaches, drains, or sampling points
captions or context about river water quality, sewage, pollution monitoring, or seasonal/monthly observations
fallback_sources: figures or graphs that clearly label location, time grouping, parameter, and numeric axis values
supplementary tables or appendix tables referenced as part of the same study dataset
narrative text only when it directly reports the same measurement values and no clearer table is available

fields.label: Sr No.
fields.description: Sequential row number. Leave null during extraction; export code will fill this field.

fields.label: Location(actual name not some legend thing)
fields.description: Actual sampling location, station, site, drain, reach, city, stretch, or monitoring point name. Prefer the real name over legend IDs.
fields.normalization: Use the actual location name from the paper. If a table only uses an ID, resolve it from the site legend or methods section.

fields.label: Date
fields.description: Specific sampling date or date range exactly as reported, if available.

fields.label: Month
fields.description: Sampling month exactly as reported, if available.

fields.label: Year
fields.description: Sampling year as a four-digit year, if available.

fields.label: Season
fields.description: Sampling season or campaign label, such as summer, winter, monsoon, pre-monsoon, or post-monsoon.

fields.label: Parameter
fields.description: Name of the water quality parameter, pollutant, microbial indicator, nutrient, or physicochemical variable.
fields.normalization: Use a clean parameter name close to the paper wording, but avoid expanding abbreviations unless the paper clearly defines them.

fields.label: Actual Value
fields.description: The reported value exactly as it appears or as a clean textual representation, including single values, ranges, BDL/ND, < values, or mean +/- SD strings.

fields.label: Mean
fields.description: Mean or average value if explicitly reported or clearly represented by a mean +/- SD cell.

fields.label: Unit
fields.description: Measurement unit exactly as reported in the paper, such as mg/L, ug/L, MPN/100 mL, CFU/100 mL, or pH units.

fields.label: Source
fields.description: Source label for where the measurement came from, such as a table, figure, graph, appendix table, supplementary table, or text section.

fields.label: Notes/Extraction Remark
fields.description: Short note about ambiguity, merged cells, transformed values, source quotes, inferred fields, or other extraction details when needed.
